skip_reason: Relies on the page's meta[name="description"] containing a ' - <name> on Instagram:' pattern and the raw document.body.innerHTML containing a 'profilePage_<id>"' marker, both of which Instagram only renders for fully server-rendered/logged-out-compatible profile pages in certain locales/conditions. In current headless CI (no session, possible login wall or React-only shell), these markers are frequently absent, leaving displayName/username/instagramId as 'Not Found'. The modal would still render with 'Not Found' placeholders, which doesn't meaningfully validate extraction, so this is skipped pending a known-good fixture.
notes: Passive - reads meta[name='description'] (format '... - DisplayName (@username) on Instagram: ...') and searches document.body.innerHTML for a 'profilePage_<id>"' marker to get the numeric Instagram user ID. Builds a draggable modal titled 'Instagram User Profile Details' with Username, Display Name, Instagram ID, current URL, and a constructed https://www.instagram.com/uid/{id} link, plus export buttons. If run manually while logged in on a real profile page, expect the modal_appears/text_present checks below to pass.

checks.comment: At least one strong element with extracted profile data (username, display name, or ID)
